extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. opted_out is set true only by t1 (optout) and never reset to false. The danger is transition t4: on send_attempt if (opted_out and acc==50): sent:=true — this sets sent=true while opted_out is true, IF acc can reach 50 after opt-out. The step events compute acc:=(3*acc+c) mod 64 (the triple nested if-then-else fully reduces into [0,64) since 3*63+53=242 needs at most 3 subtractions of 64): step_0 -> +33, step_1 -> +53, step_2 -> +23, all mod 64. These guards are 'true' and update acc regardless of opted_out. BFS from acc=0 found the path step_2,step_0,step_2,step_2 reaches acc=50 (0->23->38->9->50). Simulating the full sequence optout, step_2, step_0, step_2, step_2, send_attempt: after optout opted_out=true,sent=false,acc=0; the four steps drive acc to 50 (setting sent=false each time); then send_attempt — not opted_out is false so t2 fails, opted_out and acc!=50 is false so t3 fails, opted_out and acc==50 is true so t4 fires setting sent:=true. Final state opted_out=true AND sent=true violates the invariant. 6 events, well within the 66-event bound.
